tps.title: Unused LOG_FILE variable indicates dead code or incomplete logging
Idempotency check is race-prone and can trigger duplicate ingestions
tps.reason: Fixed — added .ingesting lock file created before backgrounding, removed after completion.

tps.title: session_id is interpolated into URL without encoding
tps.reason: Fixed — now URL-encodes via jq @uri before interpolation.

tps.title: Lock file creation is non-atomic and allows concurrent checkpoint runs
tps.reason: Real issue. Changed from touch to mkdir for atomic locking. Also caught by Gemini 3.1 Pro review.

tps.title: Full-directory find on every hook invocation can violate the never block goal
tps.reason: Real performance issue. Replaced find with direct path construction from CWD, with heartbeat cache fallback.

tps.title: Lock file is leaked on early exits, blocking future ingestion for the session
tps.reason: Real bug. Gates 4 and 5 exited without removing the .ingesting lock file, permanently blocking re-ingestion. Added rm -f before early exits.

tps.title: Predictable /tmp filenames derived from untrusted session_id enable file clobbering/symlink attacks
tps.reason: Valid security concern. Moved all state files from /tmp to ~/.claude/housemem-run/ which is user-owned and restricted.

tps.title: Predictable files in /tmp create symlink and race-condition risks
tps.reason: Same as session-summary finding. Moved all state to ~/.claude/housemem-run/.

tps.title: grep -oP not portable on macOS
tps.reason: BSD grep on macOS lacks -P (PCRE). Fixed by switching to grep -oE with ERE pattern. Verified working on macOS.

tps.title: Relative path to backfill-episodes.sh
tps.reason: Valid. Fixed by resolving SCRIPT_DIR and REPO_DIR from the script's location, then using absolute paths for all references to backfill-episodes.sh.

tps.title: Regex injection in grep project check
tps.reason: Valid. Project names like -Users-jsnyder-foo.bar would cause regex issues with grep -q. Fixed with grep -Fq for literal string matching.

tps.title: Broad grep matching for project coverage
tps.reason: Fixed as part of the grep -Fq change. Literal matching prevents substring/regex confusion.

tps.title: Percentile formula int(n*q) is off-by-one; use nearest-rank ceil(q*n)-1
tps.reason: Verified RED with synthetic xs=1..100: old formula returned index 95 for p95 (=96th item). New formula returns index 94 (=95th item). Fixed and verified GREEN.

tps.title: Manual JSON string concatenation is brittle to quotes in URL/query_file/captured_at
tps.reason: URL and file paths embedded via shell echo would break if they contained double quotes or backslashes. Replaced with `jq -n --arg` — proper JSON encoding regardless of input.

tps.title: Silent `|| continue` hides failed queries; no skip count surfaced
tps.reason: Real observability gap. If the reranker or API degrades, the snapshot would silently lose queries and drift the "quality fingerprint" without warning. Added TOTAL/SUCCEEDED/SKIPPED tracking with stderr summary and JSON fields.

fps.title: YAML uses = syntax for passwords and placeholder instead of real secret
fps.reason: Tool secret redaction replaced actual values with [REDACTED_SECRET], then flagged the redacted output. Actual file uses correct YAML password: syntax.

fps.title: Unsanitized session ID is used in a /tmp path
fps.reason: Claude Code session IDs are always UUIDs (hex + hyphens). No path traversal possible.

fps.title: Predictable /tmp counter file is vulnerable to symlink and tampering attacks
fps.reason: Single-user dev machine, not multi-tenant. Counter is informational only — tampered value has zero security impact.

fps.title: Observation data is sent over plaintext HTTP by default
fps.reason: Local network traffic to Docker VM on same subnet. All other hooks and services in this project use the same HTTP endpoint. HTTPS is unnecessary for LAN-only traffic.

fps.title: Rate limit file in /tmp is vulnerable to symlink clobbering
fps.reason: Single-user dev machine. Worst case a symlink causes rate limit to misfire — one extra or skipped observation. Zero security impact.

fps.title: Authentication header may be sent over insecure HTTP by default
fps.reason: Same as tool-observe — local network to Docker VM. All services use HTTP on LAN.
